preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiceless alveolar stop is a process of fortition and devoicing, typically occurring in syllable onsets or as part of a formal speech register in languages that use flapping as a lenition process.

examples.note: While a register shift rather than a diachronic sound law, it represents the fortition of the flap back to its underlying voiceless stop.
Children may substitute the flap with the more perceptually salient voiceless stop.

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
